title: caesalpinia trothaei subsp. erlangeri
intro: Caesalpinia trothaei subsp. erlangeri, a lesser-known species of the Caesalpinia genus, is a type of flowering plant native to Madagascar. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits, including antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. Herbal preparations of Caesalpinia trothaei subsp. erlangeri are often made by drying the leaves and roots, which are then used in tea or as a tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
